title: people_field.c is the MusyX audio runtime plus item-use code, not NPC logic
summary: 0x80146E88-0x80165400 is the MusyX runtime (seq/synth/mcmd/voice/adsr/sal/inp/hw/aram/ReverbHI); the 0x404-stride array at lbl_8047AF48 is the MusyX voice struct allocated by synthInit (0x8014D000). 0x80144574+ is item-use-on-Pokemon logic. people_data.c is the item-data module. Reference source: AxioDL/musyx runtime.
do: Port from AxioDL/musyx reference and verify against target asm.
Treat any remaining peopleField*-style naming in this range as fiction to be corrected on byte-match evidence.
do_not: Do not model NPC/people semantics onto functions in this range.
Do not trust pre-2026-07 comments in these files.

title: The real NPC/people system lives at 0x80180C78-0x8018FE30 (PeopleEntry stride 0xDC)
summary: Verified NPC entity system: PeopleEntry stride 0xDC, GS model/render callees (fn_800EC954 etc.). Old-campaign TU source archived at archive/previous_campaign/src/game/people/people.c (136 fns, ~14 real C); 49 asm extracts exist as src/game/people/people_fn_*.inc.
do: Use people.h struct offsets as hypotheses and verify widths against asm.
Keep asm includes in dead #if 0 branches only.
do_not: Do not count asm-include functions as matches.

title: effect_util.c is the msgctrl text-engine TU — apply the table-proven port names
summary: The unit at 0x80131630-0x80132690 was mis-attributed as 'effect_util' by the old transplant — it is the text-engine escape-code (msgctrl) handler TU. 76 table-proven msgctrl* names for its fn_ definitions are parked in config/GC6E01/symbolmap/msgctrl_ports.json (index-parallel port from XD's msgctrlcode table, 10/10 anchors validated). The escape-code dispatch table is lbl_80363778 (95 entries [param u32, handler ptr], consumer fn_800F98F4).
do: Apply the msgctrl_ports.json renames to the definitions you touch.
Port the handler bodies index-parallel from XD's msgctrlcode table.
do_not: Do not model generic 'effect util' semantics onto these functions.
Do not trust pre-2026-07 effect_util naming.

title: gs_title cluster hits the SDA2 anonymous-constant wall — park it, don't grind
applies_when: objdiff residual is an SDA2/@sda21 anonymous float/double constant ordering
summary: The gs_title cluster is blocked by the SDA2 anonymous-constant pairing wall (wall-sda2-anonymous-constant-CLOSED): the mismatch is MWCC's ordering of anonymous @sda21 constants, which has NO project-side source fix. This is a confirmed CLOSED ceiling — a logged attempt is fine, but escalation and grinding will not close it.
do: Make one logged attempt then park per global_standard:logged-attempt-then-escalate.
Route these to the permuter farm rather than model escalation.
do_not: Do not escalate SDA2-anonymous-constant residuals up the model ladder — the ceiling is provable.
Do not claim a match without objdiff-clean evidence.

title: game/ residuals that are pure register/schedule churn are permuter work, not model work
applies_when: objdiff residual is register allocation / instruction scheduling only, source is already faithful C
summary: Much of the remaining src/game/ residual is MWCC register-coloring / scheduling nondeterminism, not a source defect. Per park-register-schedule-walls and wall-r30-r31-swap-exhausted, decl-order has a provable ceiling on r30/r31 pair swaps. When the C is already natural and only register/schedule differs, that is the permuter's job.
do: Try the codegen_tactics levers once (decl-order, #pragma scheduling/peephole) then hand to the permuter.
Follow global_standard:logged-attempt-then-escalate: one logged attempt, then escalate or park.
do_not: Do not grind r30/r31 pair-swap residuals by hand — the decl-order ceiling is proven (wall-r30-r31-swap-exhausted).
Do not escalate pure-scheduling residuals to sol; reserve sol for source-controllable walls.

title: Permuter-won functions awaiting land — do NOT re-attack, re-decompile, or re-queue
applies_when: target symbol is one of the permuter-won-awaiting-land functions listed in do_not
about to permute/decompile a function in one of the scope_globs files
summary: The 3090 and Windows decomp-permuter farms already found a zero-diff (asm-exact) match for the functions listed in do_not (orchestrator.sqlite permuter_status.state='win'). They still read <100% fuzzy in build/GC6E01/report.json only because the won C has not been landed into the tree yet. Re-attacking them wastes farm and agent time. The winning source is in the farm outbox/results (3090: farm/results/<fn>/, Windows: pkmn-permuter/outbox/<fn>/).

The permuter queue-refresh of 2026-07-10 excludes these from both farm queues; this fact keeps worker agents from re-opening them until they land (at which point report.json fuzzy>=100 and function_status supersedes this fact).
do: If you are about to work one of these symbols, STOP and pull the already-won C from the farm outbox/results instead of re-deriving.
Prefer landing the existing win over any fresh decomp attempt.
do_not: Do not decompile, permute, or queue these permuter-won-awaiting-land symbols: fightFloorSetStatus, fightTrainerAiWazaDamage031, fn_8002509C, fn_800DD0B8, fn_801236F8, fn_8012F150, fn_80166168, fn_801666BC, fn_801CB1C4, fn_801F7530, fn_8020E488, itemGetStatus.
Do not treat their <100% fuzzy in report.json as an open near-miss — they are solved pending land.
evidence_refs: build/GC6E01/report.json fuzzy<100 (won but not yet landed)
queue-refresh 2026-07-10 (both farm queues retargeted to current near-misses)
